policy_statement: Seattle adopted a community goal to become carbon neutral by 2050 and directed creation of a plan to meet the goal (Resolution 31312).
verbatim_text: In 2011, the Mayor and City Council adopted a bold climate protection goal for our community to become carbon neutral by 2050 and directed the creation of a plan to meet the goal (Resolution 31312).
extraction_rationale: Formal city goal and directive via cited resolution; clear target year (2050) and governance action.
reasoning: The statement contains a clear, measurable outcome (“become carbon neutral”) and an explicit deadline (“by 2050”), and it is tied to an adopted Resolution (31312), which indicates a formal, binding governmental action rather than a purely aspirational mention. While it also includes a process element (“directed the creation of a plan”), the primary commitment (carbon neutral by 2050) is auditable and time-bound, meeting the strict validity rule.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: The text commits to an outcome that can be measured as an emissions/neutrality status: "become carbon neutral". Timeline: The commitment includes an explicit year: "by 2050". Binding mechanism: The action is adopted via a formal city instrument: "Mayor and City Council adopted" and cites "(Resolution 31312)", and it also "directed the creation of a plan", indicating an official directive. Spatial specificity: The policy scope is explicitly for "our community" (Seattle community-wide), i.e., it applies to the community/city jurisdiction. Weak language: Not primarily framed as 'encourage/aim/explore'; it states "adopted" a goal and "directed" plan creation. Strong language: Contains directive verbs and a hard target year: "adopted...goal...to become carbon neutral by 2050" and "directed the creation of a plan".

policy_statement: Seattle adopted a Resource Conservation Management Plan with a goal to reduce energy use across City-owned buildings by 20% by 2020 (2008 baseline).
verbatim_text: In 2013, the City adopted a Resource Conservation Management Plan (Resolution 31491), with the goal to achieve a 20 percent reduction in energy use across City-owned buildings by 2020 (2008 baseline).
extraction_rationale: Formal plan with quantified target, deadline, and baseline.
reasoning: The verbatim text describes an adopted, named plan via a City resolution that sets a measurable outcome (20% reduction in energy use) for a clearly defined asset class (City-owned buildings) by a specific year (2020) with a baseline (2008). This is an auditable commitment rather than a vague aspiration, and the resolution provides a binding/official mechanism supporting enforceability.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: "achieve a 20 percent reduction in energy use" (measurable outcome) and includes baseline "(2008 baseline)." Timeline: "by 2020" is an explicit deadline/year. Binding mechanism: "adopted ... (Resolution 31491)" indicates formal adoption via resolution. Spatial specificity: applies to a defined jurisdiction/portfolio: "across City-owned buildings" (i.e., the City’s building stock). Weak language: not primarily framed as encourage/explore; it states a goal "to achieve" a specific reduction. Strong language: contains outcome-oriented verb with hard number and deadline: "achieve a 20 percent reduction ... by 2020." Therefore STRICT VALID RULE passes (auditable commitment + timeline + quantifiable target, and no weak language / plus binding mechanism).
